It's dropped like 10% since trump took office, and they are continuing to devalue the dollar. inflation makes money worth less. The real problem arises when tech stocks start losing growth speed, because everything that goes up parabolic goes down parabolic. But for instance amazon: 2.5x EBIT growth between 2023 and 2025. text: I’d never buy Bitcoin because it has no physical form or intrinsic value; it’s just code on a computer, not a real asset. It has no intrinsic utility outside its own network: it generates no cash flow, can’t be used physically, and isn’t required for survival, industry, or manufacturing, meaning its price exists only if people agree it has value and keep trading it higher. Gold and silver, on the other hand, have thousands of years of proven value, are tangible, and don’t disappear if the internet goes down, regulations change, or a wallet gets hacked. Precious metals are universally recognized, naturally scarce, and useful beyond speculation, while Bitcoin relies on hype, consensus, and fragile systems, and gold and silver remain real stores of wealth regardless of trends or tech failures. 😆😂

text: Open a Roth IRA Buy VTI for the whole market growth SCHD for the dividends 90-80% should be VTI. 10-20% SCHD. Ppl will say you don’t need SCHD because it has overlap with VTI. Cool, SCHD is for the psychological benefit of chasing the dividends. Feels good buying a $30 stock and knowing you’re adding another dollar and some changes for annual reoccurring dividends, that you should be reinvesting back into the stock. VTI should still be the majority of the portfolio. Buy these both when the market is high and buy when it’s low. Time in the market is greater than timing the market. You don’t really need to make your portfolio more complicated than that. Super easy, super boring, max your contributions yearly, then enjoy retirement.

text: I will never understand the obsession with mega caps... Especially mega caps with huge growth already priced in. Climbing the mountain is far easier than staying at the peak. Some of these companies simply can't get any bigger because they are already in thousands of different markets and competing with smaller, more efficient companies that specialize in those markets. Nvidia has the largest market cap on Earth and has future growth priced in. Where is that growth coming from? They already dominate chips. They have expanded into robotics and all sorts of other things, but they will be competing with companies whose sole focus is robotics. They will also face competition in chips from the likes of AMD and AVGO (as well as other companies that are still small). Why not focus even on just large cap stocks like MU? The memory shortage is huge, and MU still has a PEG ratio well below 1. Or even smaller consumer-direct memory companies like WDC or Sandisk? Why put everything into companies that have already peaked?

text: I think people are overreacting & overthinking with this. I am an AI Architect. Yes. It is true that AI can definitely help to develop PoC or small applications rapidly. But, it has lots of trade off as well & there is a new article published, which stressed the enterprise level implementation limitations with all such AI tools. It is definitely a great boon for engineers. But, we observe that you really can’t completely develop & maintain a complex solution without the human intervention. Not with the existing published models. It still needs some more time. Now, guidelines to Indian tech - Innovate in products. Stop playing safe baits of body-shopping. And, start investing in product development. And, encourage more entrepreneurs to come up with new product concepts.

text: As I have been advocating for a long time, India needs to treat tariff negotiations as a high priority and move quickly to negotiate with the U.S. India is heavily dependent on U.S. capital and technology. At the same time, the U.S. is not pleased with India’s role in strengthening BRICS. It’s important to note that the U.S. does not have a problem with India maintaining bilateral relationships with Russia or China. However, forming or supporting blocs that challenge the U.S.-led financial system and the dollar is something the U.S. will respond to—often quietly and strategically. We should also remember that India is still a relatively poor country. Much of the GDP growth is driven by population growth rather than per-capita income gains, largely because there are very few high-paying jobs. My recommendation is to handle geopolitics with extreme care, especially now that we have entered the AI era, where the U.S. clearly leads the AI stack—including critical components like energy. At present, India is primarily a consumer of advanced technologies, and it will take decades to become a seller of critical technologies. Until that shift happens, our global negotiating leverage will remain limited.

text: ये सब हमारी सरकार की ग़लत नितीयो से हो रहा है।कूछ मूठठी भर बदमाश सट्टेबाजी करने वाले लोगों को सरकार को लूटने का लाईसेंस दें रखा है। सभी सरकारी पीएसयू शेयरों को सिर्फ रोकड़ा में ट्रैंड टू ट्रेड बीना सर्किट करो। सरकारी कम्पनी जो उचा डीवीडैनट ईलड है फिर इनका नाश क्यों।आर ई सी पीई 5 से कम हैईपीएस 65है।इस को कूछ मूठठी भर बदमाश सट्टेबाजी करने वाले ने 50% कम कर जनता को लूटने का काम किया है। ये आदमी बकवास कर रहा है। जनता को मूर्ख बना रहे हैं। क्या है एफ आई।सब कुछ सट्टेबाजी करने वाले लोगों का काम है। कहा है सरकार। इन सभी को तुरंत बंद किया जाना चाहिए। हर सप्ताह दो एक्सपायरी क्यों। सबको लूट रहे है मैरे 90लाख रूपया अब 65 लाख रुपए रह गया है। सबको लूट रहे है
